text:
This is not about, what language we speak, this is about inconsistency of those people who works in that building: I was diagnosed with bronchitis 5 days ago and I started using pulmicort, berodual, some syrup for cough and antibiotics. Then, as they said; I came at 8:08 am this morning to control and same doctor said to that I am too late why did I wait to come and now I am pneumonia and I should have those tests, graphic and come at 2 pm for results and medicine… And just few minutes ago I exit Dom Zdravlja because they said I need to come tomorrow morning… So I was late because you said and now I will be even more late because you say so… Good job.
Also please explain me; how can a doctor cannot talk, cannot comunicate in English? I am fully respecting to the whole culture I am living and I am studying Montenegrin every freaking day. But health system is something different… It is insane that in that city there are only 2-3 doctors who can speak english fluenty.
Ne radi se o tome kojim jezikom govorimo, radi se o nekonzistentnosti tih ljudi koji rade u toj zgradi: meni je dijagnostikovan bronhitis pre 5 dana i počela sam da koristim pulmikort, berodual, neki sirup za kašalj i antibiotike. Zatim, kako su rekli; Došla sam jutros u 8:08 na kontrolu i isti doktor mi je rekla da kasnim zašto sam čekla da dođem a sad sam upala pluća i treba da uradim te pretrage, grafičke i da dođem u 14 časova po rezultate i lekove... A pre par minuta izlazim iz Doma zdravlja jer su mi rekli da moram da dođem sutra ujutru... Pa sam zakasnila jer si rekla a sad ću još više kasniti jer ti tako kažeš... Bravo.

Then, as they said; I came at 8:08 am this morning to control and same doctor said to that I am too late why did I wait to come and now I am pneumonia and I should have those tests, graphic and come at 2 pm for results and medicine… And just few minutes ago I exit Dom Zdravlja because they said I need to come tomorrow morning… So I was late because you said and now I will be even more late because you say so… Good job.
Also please explain me; how can a doctor cannot talk, cannot comunicate in English? I am fully respecting to the whole culture I am living and I am studying Montenegrin every freaking day. But health system is something different… It is insane that in that city there are only 2-3 doctors who can speak english fluenty.
Ne radi se o tome kojim jezikom govorimo, radi se o nekonzistentnosti tih ljudi koji rade u toj zgradi: meni je dijagnostikovan bronhitis pre 5 dana i počela sam da koristim pulmikort, berodual, neki sirup za kašalj i antibiotike. Zatim, kako su rekli; Došla sam jutros u 8:08 na kontrolu i isti doktor mi je rekla da kasnim zašto sam čekla da dođem a sad sam upala pluća i treba da uradim te pretrage, grafičke i da dođem u 14 časova po rezultate i lekove... A pre par minuta izlazim iz Doma zdravlja jer su mi rekli da moram da dođem sutra ujutru... Pa sam zakasnila jer si rekla a sad ću još više kasniti jer ti tako kažeš... Bravo.
translations.sr_cyrl:
This is not about, what language we speak, this is about inconsistency of those people who works in that building: I was diagnosed with bronchitis 5 days ago and I started using pulmicort, berodual, some syrup for cough and antibiotics. Then, as they said; I came at 8:08 am this morning to control and same doctor said to that I am too late why did I wait to come and now I am pneumonia and I should have those tests, graphic and come at 2 pm for results and medicine… And just few minutes ago I exit Dom Zdravlja because they said I need to come tomorrow morning… So I was late because you said and now I will be even more late because you say so… Good job.
Also please explain me; how can a doctor cannot talk, cannot comunicate in English? I am fully respecting to the whole culture I am living and I am studying Montenegrin every freaking day. But health system is something different… It is insane that in that city there are only 2-3 doctors who can speak english fluenty.
Не ради се о томе којим језиком говоримо, ради се о неконзистентности тих људи који раде у тој згради: мени је дијагностикован бронхитис пре 5 дана и почела сам да користим пулмикорт, беродуал, неки сируп за кашаљ и антибиотике. Затим, како су рекли; Дошла сам јутрос у 8:08 на контролу и исти доктор ми је рекла да касним зашто сам чекла да дођем а сад сам упала плућа и треба да урадим те претраге, графичке и да дођем у 14 часова по резултате и лекове... А пре пар минута излазим из Дома здравља јер су ми рекли да морам да дођем сутра ујутру... Па сам закаснила јер си рекла а сад ћу још више каснити јер ти тако кажеш... Браво.
translations.en:
This is not about what language we speak, this is about the inconsistency of those people who work in that building: I was diagnosed with bronchitis 5 days ago and I started using pulmicort, berodual, some syrup for cough and antibiotics. Then, as they said; I came at 8:08 am this morning for a check-up and the same doctor said that I am too late, why did I wait to come, and now I have pneumonia and I should have those tests, x-ray, and come at 2 pm for results and medicine... And just a few minutes ago I exited Dom Zdravlja because they said I need to come tomorrow morning... So I was late because you said so and now I will be even more late because you say so... Good job.
Also please explain to me; how can a doctor not talk, not communicate in English? I fully respect the whole culture I am living in and I am studying Montenegrin every single day. But the health system is something different... It is insane that in that city there are only 2-3 doctors who can speak English fluently.
It's not about what language we speak, it's about the inconsistency of those people who work in that building: I was diagnosed with bronchitis 5 days ago and I started using pulmicort, berodual, some cough syrup and antibiotics. Then, as they said; I came this morning at 8:08 for a check-up and the same doctor told me I was late, why did I wait to come, and now I have pneumonia and I need to do those tests, x-rays and come at 2 pm for results and medications... And a few minutes ago I'm leaving Dom Zdravlja because they told me I have to come tomorrow morning... So I was late because you said so, and now I'll be even more late because you say so... Bravo.
translations.ru:
Дело не в том, на каком языке мы говорим, дело в непоследовательности тех людей, которые работают в этом здании: мне диагностировали бронхит 5 дней назад, и я начала принимать пульмикорт, беродуал, какой-то сироп от кашля и антибиотики. Затем, как они сказали; я пришла в 8:08 утра на контроль, и тот же врач сказала, что я опоздала, зачем ждала, и теперь у меня пневмония, и мне нужно сдать анализы, рентген и прийти в 14 часов за результатами и лекарствами... А несколько минут назад я выхожу из Дома здравля, потому что сказали прийти завтра утром... То есть я опоздала, потому что ты так сказала, а теперь опоздаю ещё больше, потому что ты так говоришь... Молодцы.
А ещё объясните мне: как может врач не говорить, не общаться на английском? Я полностью уважаю культуру, в которой живу, и каждый день учу черногорский. Но система здравоохранения — это другое... Безумие, что в этом городе только 2-3 врача свободно говорят по-английски.
Не в том дело, на каком языке мы говорим, а в непоследовательности тех людей, которые работают в том здании: мне диагностировали бронхит 5 дней назад, и я начала использовать пульмикорт, беродуал, какой-то сироп от кашля и антибиотики. Затем, как они сказали; я пришла утром в 8:08 на контроль, и тот же врач мне сказала, что я опоздала, зачем ждала, а теперь у меня воспаление лёгких, и нужно сделать анализы, рентген и прийти в 14 часов за результатами и лекарствами... А несколько минут назад выхожу из Дома здравля, потому что сказали прийти завтра утром... Значит я опоздала, потому что ты сказала, а теперь ещё больше опоздаю, потому что ты так говоришь... Браво.
translations.de:
Es geht nicht darum, welche Sprache wir sprechen, es geht um die Inkonsequenz der Leute, die in diesem Gebäude arbeiten: Mir wurde vor 5 Tagen Bronchitis diagnostiziert und ich begann Pulmicort, Berodual, einen Hustensaft und Antibiotika zu nehmen. Dann, wie sie sagten; ich kam heute Morgen um 8:08 Uhr zur Kontrolle und dieselbe Ärztin sagte, ich sei zu spät, warum ich gewartet hätte, und jetzt hätte ich eine Lungenentzündung und müsse diese Tests machen, Röntgen, und um 14 Uhr für Ergebnisse und Medikamente kommen... Und vor ein paar Minuten verlasse ich Dom Zdravlja, weil sie sagten, ich müsse morgen früh kommen... Also war ich zu spät, weil Sie es so sagten, und jetzt werde ich noch später dran sein, weil Sie es so sagen... Gute Arbeit.
Erklären Sie mir bitte auch; wie kann ein Arzt nicht auf Englisch sprechen, nicht kommunizieren? Ich respektiere die gesamte Kultur, in der ich lebe, voll und ganz und lerne jeden Tag Montenegrinisch. Aber das Gesundheitssystem ist etwas anderes... Es ist verrückt, dass es in dieser Stadt nur 2-3 Ärzte gibt, die fließend Englisch sprechen.
Es geht nicht darum, welche Sprache wir sprechen, es geht um die Inkonsequenz der Leute, die in dem Gebäude arbeiten: mir wurde vor 5 Tagen Bronchitis diagnostiziert und ich begann Pulmicort, Berodual, einen Hustensaft und Antibiotika zu verwenden. Dann, wie sie sagten; ich kam heute Morgen um 8:08 zur Kontrolle und dieselbe Ärztin sagte mir, ich sei zu spät, warum ich gewartet hätte, und jetzt hätte ich eine Lungenentzündung und müsse die Untersuchungen machen, Röntgen, und um 14 Uhr für Ergebnisse und Medikamente kommen... Und vor ein paar Minuten verlasse ich das Dom Zdravlja, weil sie sagten, ich müsse morgen früh kommen... Also war ich zu spät, weil du es gesagt hast, und jetzt werde ich noch mehr zu spät sein, weil du es sagst... Bravo.
translations.tr:
Bu hangi dili konuştuğumuzla ilgili değil, bu o binada çalışan insanların tutarsızlığıyla ilgili: 5 gün önce bronşit teşhisi kondu ve pulmicort, berodual, öksürük şurubu ve antibiyotik kullanmaya başladım. Sonra, dedikleri gibi; bu sabah 8:08'de kontrole geldim ve aynı doktor geç kaldığımı, neden beklediğimi söyledi, şimdi zatürrem var ve bu testleri, röntgeni yaptırıp saat 14:00'te sonuçlar ve ilaçlar için gelmem gerekiyor... Ve birkaç dakika önce Dom Zdravlja'dan çıkıyorum çünkü yarın sabah gelmem gerektiğini söylediler... Yani sen söylediğin için geç kaldım, şimdi de sen öyle dediğin için daha da geç kalacağım... Aferin.
Ayrıca bana açıklayın; bir doktor nasıl İngilizce konuşamaz, iletişim kuramaz? Yaşadığım kültüre tamamen saygı duyuyorum ve her gün Karadağca çalışıyorum. Ama sağlık sistemi farklı bir şey... Bu şehirde sadece 2-3 doktorun akıcı İngilizce konuşabilmesi çılgınlık.
Hangi dilde konuştuğumuzla ilgili değil, o binada çalışan insanların tutarsızlığıyla ilgili: 5 gün önce bronşit teşhisi kondu ve pulmicort, berodual, öksürük şurubu ve antibiyotik kullanmaya başladım. Sonra, dedikleri gibi; bu sabah 8:08'de kontrole geldim ve aynı doktor bana geç kaldığımı, neden beklediğimi söyledi, şimdi zatürrem var ve o tetkikleri, röntgeni yaptırıp saat 14:00'te sonuçlar ve ilaçlar için gelmem gerekiyor... Ve birkaç dakika önce Dom Zdravlja'dan çıkıyorum çünkü yarın sabah gelmem gerektiğini söylediler... Yani sen söylediğin için geç kaldım, şimdi de sen öyle dediğin için daha da geç kalacağım... Bravo.
